_sources: Nominatim/OSM geocoding 2026-07-16 (Holter Dam, Craig, Wolf Creek)
Photo EXIF anchors from the trip
Debrief transcripts (launch time, boats at the dam, islands, beaver dam flat)

features.name: Holter Dam
features.note: Nominatim. Launch was the FAS ramp just below the dam — 'up at the boat by 7 o'clock... massive numbers of boats up at the dam.' Both mornings' first photos sit ~600 m downstream.

features.name: Morning riser bank
features.note: Photo-anchored (IMG_1708/1728/1826 within ~30 m across BOTH days). Crossed immediately after launch to rising fish; Thorne's Trico rainbow day 1, Luyen's long-cast Trico rainbow day 2. The Trico-cloud photo is here.

features.name: Day 1 midday water
features.note: IMG_1051 (Luyen's hopper-dropper cut from the boat, Mon 1:31 PM). The single-big-island reach with the PhD fish is this general stretch — exact island unconfirmed.

features.name: Two-island riffle + beaver-dam flat
features.note: IMG_1831 (Tue 12:42 PM, the last-and-favorite rainbow from the inside braid). Transcript: 'two islands with a big riffle section on the left' then 'downstream of the beaver dam... dry fly to sipping fish' to end the trip. Photo pins the braid; the beaver-dam flat itself is unpinned.

features.name: Wolf Creek (village)
features.note: Nominatim. The Wolf Creek Bridge FAS is between the dam and Craig; exact ramp point not independently verified — shown as approximate if at all.

features.name: Craig
features.note: Nominatim; trip photo IMG_1687 (Sun 8:01 PM, Thorne under the bug cloud) lands 25 m from this point. Lodging: The Trout Shop. Presumed take-out (unconfirmed).

float_notes: Launched at the Holter Dam ramp both days (transcript + photo clusters). Take-out not documented — likely Craig FAS given lodging, marked unconfirmed. Per Luyen's direction the site shows photo points only, NO fabricated float path.
